title: EEG, ECG and pupil data from young and older adults: rest and auditory cued reaction time tasks
description: This dataset comprises synchronized electroencephalogram (EEG), electrocardiogram (ECG), and pupillography recordings from 75 participants (36 young and 39 older adults) during auditory cued reaction time tasks and passive listening conditions. Participants performed a simple reaction time task and a go/no-go task, with EEG acquired at 500 Hz using 64 scalp electrodes, ECG recorded bipolarly from chest electrodes, and pupil data sampled at 240 Hz. The dataset is designed to investigate age-related differences in neural, cardiac, and pupillary responses during cognitive and perceptual decision-making tasks.
methods_description: EEG was recorded using a 64-channel Neuroscan system with electrodes placed according to the International 10-20 standard at 500 Hz sampling rate. ECG was recorded bipolarly from chest electrodes. Pupil data was acquired using an iView X Hi-Speed 1250 system (SMI) at 240 Hz. Participants performed auditory cued simple reaction time and go/no-go tasks (two 8-minute runs each, 120 trials per task) and a passive listening condition (4 minutes). Auditory stimuli were pure tones at 1500 Hz (cue), 1700 Hz (go), 1300 Hz (no-go), and 1000 Hz (error feedback), presented at approximately 67 dB(A). Electrode positions were digitized using a 3D Fastrak system.
